data.title: Kontrol Technologies Announces Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results
data.description: Toronto-based Kontrol Technologies Corp. (CBOE.CA:KNR) (OTCQB:KNRLF) (FSE:1K8) releases its financial results for the three months and fiscal year ending December 31, 2025. Reports can be found on SEDAR's website. In the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2025, Kontrol announced highlights, including the sale of operational net assets in the second quarter of 2024.

data.title: Iyuno Announces a Revolutionary Innovation in Global Content Localization and Beyond
data.description: On March 31, 2026, in Burbank, California, Iyuno, a top media localization services provider, unveiled CLOE, a groundbreaking contextual intelligence platform. This innovative tool aims to revolutionize content preparation, marketing, localization, and adaptation processes.

data.title: Impulse Group Condemns SCOTUS Decision Undermining Protections for LGBTQ+ Youth
data.description: In Los Angeles, advocacy group Impulse Group strongly criticizes the recent Supreme Court ruling that erodes safeguards for LGBTQ+ adolescents. The decision has sparked concern among communities, with numbers estimating approximately 1.4 million LGBTQ+ youths facing potential consequences due to this judgement. Impulse Group expresses deep disappointment, emphasizing the significance of protecting the rights and well-being of young individuals within the LGBTQ+ community.

data.title: Abbisko Therapeutics' FGFR4 Inhibitor Irpagratinib Granted Orphan Drug Designation by EMA for Hepatocellular Carcinoma
data.description: On March 31, 2026, Abbisko Therapeutics Co., Ltd. revealed that their oral small-molecule FGFR4 inhibitor, irpagratinib (ABSK-011), received Orphan Drug Designation. Abbisko Therapeutics, traded under the HKEX code 02256, developed this highly selective treatment.

data.title: Beyond the Build: Five Robotics Practices That Connect Students to Purpose, Partners, and Possibility
data.description: In Boston on March 31, 2026, it was reported that numerous schools throughout the United States provide robotics programs either as a technical elective or club focusing on coding, constructing, and competing. However, as the economy continues to rapidly change, there is a growing demand for additional educational opportunities in this field. Robotics is a multidisciplinary field that incorporates computer science, engineering, and technology, highlighting the need for a comprehensive approach to education in this area.
